summary: A single-storey rectangular stone building with a stepped dark-red (netherbrick) gable-like roof, a low cobblestone perimeter foundation, horizontal row windows on each long side, and stone slab accents on roof steps and corners.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with a raised stepped roof running along the long axis
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the central longitudinal axis

elements: continuous horizontal windows (3-4 per long side) set one block above the lower stonebrick course
single-storey interior implied (no visible upstairs windows or dormers)
stepped netherbrick roof with stone slab accents on each step and at roof corners
cobblestone surround one block high around the base forming a shallow moat/ledge
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular stonebrick wall of about 11x19 blocks footprint and 3-4 blocks tall for the main wall.
Place a one-block-high cobblestone border around the outside perimeter flush with the ground (serves as foundation/ledge).
Install a continuous row of glass panes/windows centered horizontally on both long walls (three windows separated by one stonebrick column each).
Construct a symmetrical stepped netherbrick roof along the long axis: multiple layers of netherbrick stairs/slabs rising to a short ridge; add stone slabs at the outer corners of each step.
Ensure roof overhang is minimal; roof top is flat/ridge about 1-2 blocks wide.
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and door location are not visible; entrance may be on a short side or obscured.
Precise block counts for width/depth/height are estimated from perspective images and top views; could be off by a few blocks.
Material identification for roof accent slabs is based on color/shape and assumed to be stone slabs; could be a different light-gray slab (e.g., smooth stone).
